parameters.jsCode:
const items = $input.all();

return items.map((item) => {
  const raw = item.json ?? {};

  // support both structures
  // data 1 => raw.data.data
  // data 2 => raw.data
  const root =
    raw?.data?.data ||
    raw?.data ||
    {};

  const product =
    root?.Detail_product ??
    null;

  const seller =
    root?.Detail_seller?.data ??
    root?.Detail_seller ??
    null;

  return {
    json: {
      // PRODUCT
      url: product?.url ?? null,

      productTitle: product?.title ?? null,
      productSummary: product?.aiSummary ?? null,
      productDescription: product?.description ?? null,

      favoritesCount: product?.favoritesCount ?? null,

      // SPECIFICATIONS
      specEra:
        product?.specifications?.Era ?? null,

      specBrand:
        product?.specifications?.Brand ?? null,

      specModel:
        product?.specifications?.Model ||
        product?.specifications?.["Model/name"] ||
        null,

      specArtist:
        product?.specifications?.Artist ?? null,

      specDesigner:
        product?.specifications?.["Designer/artist/maker"] ?? null,

      specManufacturer:
        product?.specifications?.["Manufacturer/brand"] ?? null,

      specCountryOrigin:
        product?.specifications?.["Country of origin"] ?? null,

      specMaterial:
        product?.specifications?.Material ?? null,

      specStyle:
        product?.specifications?.Style ?? null,

      specColour:
        product?.specifications?.Colour ?? null,

      specCondition:
        product?.specifications?.Condition ?? null,

      specPeriod:
        product?.specifications?.Period ?? null,

      specYear:
        product?.specifications?.Year ?? null,

      specTechnique:
        product?.specifications?.Technique ?? null,

      specSignature:
        product?.specifications?.Signature ?? null,

      specEdition:
        product?.specifications?.Edition ?? null,

      specSoldBy:
        product?.specifications?.["Sold by"] ?? null,

      specSoldWithFrame:
        product?.specifications?.["Sold with frame"] ?? null,

      specTitleArtwork:
        product?.specifications?.["Title of artwork"] ?? null,

      specEstPeriod:
        product?.specifications?.["Estimated period"] ?? null,

      specHeight:
        product?.specifications?.Height ?? null,

      specWidth:
        product?.specifications?.Width ?? null,

      specDepth:
        product?.specifications?.Depth ?? null,

      specSeatingHeight:
        product?.specifications?.["Seating height"] ?? null,

      specNumObjects:
        product?.specifications?.["Number of objects"] ?? null,

      // SHIPPING
      shippingDestination:
        product?.shipping?.destination ?? null,

      shippingCost:
        product?.shipping?.cost ?? null,

      pickupLocation:
        product?.shipping?.pickupLocation ?? null,

      // IMAGES
      imageUrls: (product?.images ?? [])
        .map(img => img?.url)
        .filter(Boolean)
        .join(" | ") || null,

      imageAlts: (product?.images ?? [])
        .map(img => img?.alt)
        .filter(Boolean)
        .join(" | ") || null,

      // SELLER (FROM PRODUCT)
      sellerName:
        product?.seller?.name ?? null,

      sellerProfileUrl:
        product?.seller?.profileUrl ?? null,

      sellerCountry: seller?.country ??
        product?.seller?.country ??  null,

      sellerVerified:
        product?.seller?.verified ?? null,

      sellerType:
        product?.seller?.type ?? null,

      sellerObjectsSold:
        product?.seller?.stats?.["Objects sold"] ?? null,

      sellerReviews:
        product?.seller?.stats?.["Reviews"] ?? null,

      sellerPositiveScore:
        product?.seller?.stats?.["100%"] ?? null,

      // SELLER DETAIL
      sellerId:
        seller?.sellerId ?? null,

      seller_page_url:
        seller?.sellerUrl ?? null,

      sellerCanonicalUrl:
        seller?.canonicalUrl ?? null,

      sellerDetailCountry:
        seller?.country ?? null,

      business_name:
        seller?.businessName ?? null,

      business_address:
        seller?.businessAddress ?? null,

      business_license_number:
        seller?.businessRegNumber ?? null,

      sellerReviewCount:
        seller?.reviewCount ?? null,

      sellerObjectsSoldDetail:
        seller?.objectsSold ?? null,

      sellerPositiveScoreDetail:
        seller?.positiveScore ?? null,

      reviewsUrl:
        seller?.reviewsUrl ?? null,

      sellerTags: (seller?.tags ?? [])
        .filter(Boolean)
        .join(" | ") || null,

      sellerLotLabels: (seller?.lots ?? [])
        .map(l => l?.label)
        .filter(Boolean)
        .join(" | ") || null,

      sellerLotCounts: (seller?.lots ?? [])
        .map(l => l?.count)
        .filter(v => v !== undefined && v !== null)
        .join(" | ") || null,

      sellerLotUrls: (seller?.lots ?? [])
        .map(l => l?.url)
        .filter(Boolean)
        .join(" | ") || null,
    },
  };
});

nodes.name: Check is New or Cached is available1

parameters.content:
## Spedire Lead Enrichment — Setup & Configuration

### Setup first before running this workflow
This workflow needs Google Sheets, MrScraper scrapers, MrScraper API access, and a SERP API connection before it can run correctly. After setup, the workflow will take marketplace product URLs, enrich them with product and seller data, discover the seller’s likely company website, find the contact page, and extract emails/phone numbers into Google Sheets.

---

### 1) Google Sheets setup

Create or duplicate **two Google Sheets** before running the workflow.

**A) Product URL Queue Sheet**

This sheet is the input queue for product pages you want to process.

Required columns:
- `Url` → marketplace product detail URL to scrape
- `status` → leave blank for new rows; the workflow updates this to `finished` after processing

Example:
- `Url`: product detail page URL
- `status`: blank / `finished`

**B) Leads Output Sheet**

This sheet stores the enriched lead results.

Recommended columns:
- `url`
- `company_url`
- `contact_url`
- `productTitle`
- `productSummary`
- `productDescription`
- `Specification`
- `Shipping Detail`
- `sellerName`
- `sellerCountry`
- `seller_page_url`
- `business_name`
- `business_address`
- `business_license_number`
- `emails`
- `phones`

Connect both sheets using your **Google Sheets OAuth2 credential** in n8n.

---

### 2) MrScraper credentials setup

Add your **MrScraper API credential** in n8n and connect it to all MrScraper nodes.

You also need a **MrScraper API token** for Code nodes that check previous scraper results/cache through the MrScraper Results API. This lets the workflow avoid scraping the same URL again when cached data already exists.

Replace placeholder values such as:
- `YOUR_MRSCRAPER_API_TOKEN`
- MrScraper credential placeholders
- Any scraper IDs if you create your own scrapers

---

### 3) Create MrScraper scraper: Product Detail & Seller Detail

Create a MrScraper scraper for product pages.

**Input**
- Product detail URL from the Google Sheets queue

**Expected output**
The scraper should return two main objects:

**A) Detail Product**
Recommended fields:
- product URL
- title
- description
- favorites count
- specifications such as brand, model, designer, manufacturer, country of origin, material, style, colour, condition, period/year, dimensions
- shipping destination, shipping cost, pickup location

**B) Seller Detail**
Recommended fields:
- seller ID
- seller URL
- canonical seller URL
- country
- business name
- business address
- business registration/license number

This workflow uses this scraper to enrich each product row and write product + seller details into the Leads Output Sheet.

---

### 4) SERP API setup — discover company URL

Set up your SERP API endpoint/token in the HTTP Request node.

**Input**
- Seller name / business name
- Seller address or seller country

The workflow searches Google using a query like:

`sellerName + sellerAddress`

or, if address is unavailable:

`sellerName + sellerCountry`

**Expected output**
- A list of Google search results

The workflow then scores and filters those results to find the most likely official company website.

It avoids common non-company domains such as:
- Facebook
- Instagram
- LinkedIn
- Amazon
- eBay
- Etsy
- Catawiki
- Trustpilot
- Wikipedia
- Google
- marketplace/review/social platforms

**Final output**
- `company_url`

This value is saved back into the Leads Output Sheet.

---

### 5) Create MrScraper scraper: Contact URL Finder

Create a MrScraper scraper that receives a company website and finds the most likely contact page.

**Input**
- `company_url`

**Expected behavior**
The scraper should inspect common navigation areas such as:
- header menu
- footer menu
- contact links
- about/company pages
- customer service links
- legal/business information links

**Expected output**
- `contact_url`

Examples of valid contact URLs (You can modify the pattern):
- `/contact`
- `/contacts`
- `/contact-us`
- `/contatti`
- `/about`
- `/customer-service`
- footer/header contact link

This workflow saves the discovered `contact_url` into the Leads Output Sheet.

---

### 6) Create MrScraper scraper: Contact Detail Scraper

Create a MrScraper scraper that extracts contact information from the contact page.

**Input**
- `contact_url`

**Expected output**
- `emails`
- `phones`

Optional useful fields:
- business name
- address
- VAT/tax number
- social links
- contact form URL

The workflow checks cached MrScraper results first. If the contact detail has already been scraped before, it reuses the cached result instead of running the scraper again.

---

### 7) Optional n8n execution guard
This workflow includes a duplicate-execution guard that checks running executions using the n8n API. Use this only if you want to prevent multiple runs of the same workflow at the same time.

Required value:
- `YOUR_N8N_API_KEY`
- `YOUR_WORKFLOW_ID`
- your n8n instance API URL

If you do not need this feature, you can remove or disable the duplicate-execution check section.

---

### Values you must replace after import

Replace these placeholders before running:
- `YOUR_PRODUCT_URL_SHEET_ID`
- `YOUR_LEADS_SHEET_ID`
- `YOUR_WORKFLOW_ID`
- `YOUR_N8N_API_KEY`
- `YOUR_MRSCRAPER_API_TOKEN`
- SERP API token / endpoint
- Product Detail & Seller Detail scraper ID
- Contact URL Finder scraper ID
- Contact Detail scraper ID
- Google Sheets credential
- MrScraper credential

---

### Community publishing safety

Before publishing this workflow to the n8n Community, make sure you do not include:
- private API keys
- MrScraper tokens
- SERP API tokens
- cookies
- Google Sheet IDs
- credential IDs or credential names
- private n8n instance URLs
- real workflow IDs
- private customer or lead data

Use placeholders for all sensitive values so other users can safely import and configure the workflow with their own accounts.

### Phase 1 — What happens here
1. The workflow starts manually from **Execute workflow**.
2. The **Check If there's execution** code node calls the n8n executions API and counts running executions for this workflow.
3. **Run if only one execution exists** only lets the workflow continue when the running count equals `1`.

### Why this matters
This prevents two active runs from updating the same Google Sheet rows or scraping the same URLs at the same time.

### Setup note
Replace the n8n API URL, workflow ID, and API key placeholders before enabling this guard in your own instance.
## Phase 2 — Product URL Queue & Product Detail Scraping

### Phase 2 — What happens here
1. **Get Product url** reads product URLs from the Product URL Queue Google Sheet.
2. **Loop Over Product Url** processes rows in batches of 100.
3. **Check Cached Product Detail Result** checks MrScraper result history for each product URL.
4. If cached product detail exists, the workflow reuses it. If not, **Run Detail Product Scraper** runs the product detail scraper.
5. **Merge Product Detail Data** joins cached and newly scraped outputs into one path.

### Cache behavior
The cache check looks for a valid `Detail_product.title`. It also handles MrScraper responses where the result is too large by fetching the result detail endpoint.

### Output from this phase
The next phase receives normalized product and seller detail data, whether it came from cache or a fresh scrape.
## Phase 3 — Normalize Product + Seller Data into Leads Sheet

### Phase 3 — What happens here
1. **Formatting Data** flattens nested MrScraper output into clean spreadsheet columns.
2. It extracts product information, specs, shipping data, images, seller profile, business details, seller stats, and lot/category data.
3. **Append or update Product Detail in Sheets** writes the normalized row into the Leads Google Sheet using `url` as the matching key.
4. **Append or update url's scraped status** updates the source queue sheet and marks the product URL as `finished`.

### Key fields created
- Product: `productTitle`, `productSummary`, `productDescription`, images, favorites count
- Specs: era, brand, model, material, condition, style, dimensions, year, period
- Seller: seller name, seller URL, seller country, verified/type/stats
- Business: `business_name`, `business_address`, `business_license_number`

### Resume-safe design
Because product rows are append-or-updated and queue rows are marked `finished`, the workflow can continue from where it left off after interruption.
## Phase 4 — Discover the Seller Company Website

### Phase 4 — What happens here
1. **Get sellerDetail filter by Seller Country** reads rows from the Leads Sheet where `sellerCountry = Italy`.
2. **Filter License Exists & Company URL Not Yet Available** keeps rows that have useful business/license data but have no `company_url` yet.
3. **Loop SERP Company URL by Seller Detail** processes candidates in batches of 500 (the batch size 500 because of many of them sometimes getting filtered).
4. **request SERP API** searches Google using seller/business name + country.
5. **Filter Legit Company Profile Url** scores search results and selects the most likely official company website.
6. **Append or update company Url** saves the selected `company_url` back into the Leads Sheet.
7. **Merge Serp Result** keeps the loop moving after update/error paths.

### Website scoring logic
The code removes noisy domains like social media, marketplaces, review sites, Google/Bing, Wikipedia, Amazon, eBay, Etsy, and Catawiki. It then scores candidates by domain token match, substring match, full-name match, title/snippet match, homepage bonus, search rank, and blog/builder penalties.

### Confidence control
Only candidates with a score above the threshold are saved. Adjust `CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD` inside the scoring code if you want stricter or looser matching.
## Phase 5 — Find Contact Page from Company Website

### Phase 5 — What happens here
1. **Get row(s) Company Url filterBy sellerCountry** loads lead rows from the Leads Sheet.
2. **Loop Scrape ContactUrl** batches rows with company URLs.
3. **Filter Company Url Exists & Contact Url Not Yet Available** keeps rows where `company_url` exists but `contact_url` is still empty.
4. **Run Contact Url Scraper** uses MrScraper to find likely contact/about/contact-us pages from the company website.
5. **Flatten Data** chooses the best returned URL. It prioritizes URLs with deeper paths and longer strings because contact pages are often nested.
6. **Append or update row in Contact Url** writes the selected `contact_url` into the Leads Sheet.
7. **Merge** connects the contact-url discovery section into the next enrichment path.

### Expected result
Each valid company website gets one best `contact_url`, ready for the final email/phone extraction step.
## Phase 6 — Scrape Email & Phone from Contact Pages

### Phase 6 — What happens here
1. **Get ContactUrl** reads rows from the Leads Sheet.
2. **Loop Scrape Detail Contact** batches contact-page rows.
3. **Filter Contact Url Exists & Contact Not Yet Available** keeps rows where `contact_url` exists but `phones` is empty.
4. **Check Cached Result Contact Detail1** checks MrScraper result history for the contact URL before scraping again.
5. **Check is New or Cached is available1** decides whether to run a fresh scrape.
6. **Run Contact Detail Scraper** extracts emails and phone numbers from the contact page when no cache is available.
7. **Append or update Contact Detail** and **Append or update Contact Detail1** write cached or freshly scraped `emails` and `phones` into the Leads Sheet.
8. **Merge Contact Detail Result** completes the batch loop.

### Cache-first design
This phase avoids re-running the contact-detail scraper when cached email data already exists for the same contact URL.

### Final output
The Leads Sheet ends with enriched rows containing product data, seller/business data, company website, contact page, email addresses, and phone numbers.

## Troubleshooting & Customization

### Common setup checks
- Confirm both Google Sheets contain the exact column names used in the node mappings.
- Confirm the MrScraper scraper IDs match the correct scraper type and Output structured:
  - Product Detail Scraper
  - Contact URL Scraper
  - Contact Detail Scraper
- Replace all placeholder API tokens and credentials after importing.
- Test with 1–3 rows first before running large batches.

### Tuning batch size
Current batch sizes:
- Product URLs: 100
- SERP/company lookup: 500
- Contact URL/detail flows: 500

Lower the batch size if you hit API rate limits, Google Sheets quota limits, or scraper timeouts.

### Improving company URL accuracy
Edit the blacklist and scoring weights inside **Filter Legit Company Profile Url**. Increase `CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD` for stricter matching, or lower it if too many valid sellers are skipped.

### Publishing to n8n Community
This workflow demonstrates cache-first scraping, sheet-based queueing, SERP scoring, and lead enrichment. Keep placeholders in the public version and ask users to add their own credentials and scraper IDs.
